title: supply-chain-guard policy config
description: Schema for .supply-chain-guard.yml (also .supply-chain-guard.yaml, .scg.yml, .scg.yaml). Mirrors exactly what the built-in parser in src/policy-engine.ts understands. Reference it in your config with a leading comment line: # yaml-language-server: $schema=./node_modules/supply-chain-guard/policy-schema.json

rules.description: Rule-level tuning: disable rules entirely or override their severity.

disable.description: Rule ids to disable entirely. Disabled findings are dropped and counted as suppressed.

severityOverrides.description: Map of rule id to the severity it should be reported with.

allowlist.description: Entities that are trusted and excluded from specific checks.

packages.description: Package names excluded from typosquat and dependency-confusion checks (TYPOSQUAT_LEVENSHTEIN, DEP_INTERNAL_NAME_PUBLIC).

suppress.description: Reviewed, accepted findings. Every entry needs the exact rule id and a reason as audit trail; entries without a reason are reported as POLICY_SUPPRESSION_NO_REASON.

rule.description: Exact rule id as reported by the scanner (SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE).

reason.description: Why this finding is an accepted tradeoff (audit trail).

baseline.description: Baseline diffing: only findings not present in the baseline file are reported.

file.description: Path to the baseline JSON file, relative to the scan root (or absolute).
